claim_summary: Header says all claims are machine-checked and exact.
evidence: The same document names two analytic obligations at lines 14-18.
research/derivations/legendre_native.py currently fails at Stage 3 because verify_local_curvature_calculus is unavailable.
required_review: Map each prose claim to replay coverage and narrow the header or supply a self-contained verifier.

claim_summary: Index leads with machine-checked derivation but later says the script is not run in-repo because its helper is non-portable.
evidence: The disclosure is present, but retrieval snippets can surface line 8 without lines 11-13.
A current run fails at the disclosed missing dependency.
required_review: Lead with historical partial-verifier status and list the exact replayable stages.

claim_summary: Report says CCE-5 through CCE-8 were audited without naming the repository regions or source closure searched.
evidence: A later repository-wide pass found material support that changed the CCE-5, CCE-7, and CCE-8 characterization.
Recovered sources were outside the initially prioritized live theorem paths.
required_review: State the exact audit closure or narrow the wording to campaign-designated sources.

claim_summary: StageDependencyUnavailable can be read as missing mathematics while the body says exact formulas and a recertifier exist but canonical designation is absent.
evidence: archive/Reference_Material/papers/current/dbp_orbit_calculus.tex contains the theorem body.
engine/src/cella/reference_lift.py and research/verification/recert_role_channels.py provide implementation and replay support.
required_review: Use a source-promotion and certificate-integration blocker name that matches the body.

claim_summary: Two gates in a checker described as exact decide branch ordering via floating approx calls.
evidence: K6 and K7 compare branch_1.approx() and branch_2.approx().
Other identities in the checker use exact Fraction and Q2 arithmetic.
required_review: Replace K6 and K7 with exact Q(sqrt(2)) sign comparisons or exclude them from the exact certificate count.

claim_summary: The surface theorem defines admissible corridors by choosing neighborhoods with required avoidance after explicitly stating the certified corridor is curve-level only.
evidence: Line 1555 says the curve statement does not certify a surface sweep.
Lines 1559-1569 assume thin neighborhoods avoiding the surface divisors.
required_review: Attach an explicit unproved surface-clearance hypothesis id and ensure all summaries preserve the conditional scope.

claim_summary: Header promises close-ritual freshness, but active-work scope does not explain whether user-local campaigns such as CCE should appear.
evidence: The user supplied an active CCE campaign not listed under Active work.
CURRENT.md does disclose that RESULTS and DEFINITIONS are population-pending.
required_review: Define global versus user-task scope and add a campaign manifest rather than expanding the one-screen current file.

claim_summary: Historical origin document uses promoted and established status, which can be mistaken for current STATE authority.
evidence: STATE/RESULTS.md has no migrated R5 entry.
STATE/CONSTRAINTS.md C-003 requires fresh in-repo evidence before current load-bearing status.
required_review: Preserve historical wording but expose origin_status and state_canon_status as separate retrieval fields.

claim_summary: The handoff locates durable doctrine in Claude memory rather than a reviewable repository record.
evidence: The claimed authority is inaccessible model memory
The same sentence calls the handoff perishable state
No scoped C-id or durable decision id is cited
normative_effect: Later agents may inherit hidden rules that suppress mathematical routes
required_review: Extract each durable proposition into a proof, result, scoped constraint, or open hypothesis; treat all remainder as non-normative session history.

claim_summary: An architecture summary labels results closed and says they must not be reopened, then includes a negative search claim.
evidence: The source is outside STATE/CONSTRAINTS.md
No applicable C-id or displacement rule is cited
The wording can prevent proof-scope inspection and counterevidence
One listed negative says no second surface region has been found
origin_evidence: result labels without a scoped constraint decision
normative_effect: Agents may refuse to inspect or challenge mathematical conclusions
required_review: Replace the reopening ban with result ids, exact scope, certificate links, and an explicit counterevidence or stronger-certificate displacement path.

claim_summary: A prior-art sweep is declared out of scope per Will without a durable decision id or lifetime.
evidence: A companion handoff repeats novelty and prior art as out of scope by your instruction
The campaign context suggests a local deferral but later applicability is not encoded
No C-id or dated decision record is cited
origin_evidence: personal attribution in two artifacts; original context unavailable
normative_effect: Later agents may misread a campaign-local deferral as a permanent ban on prior-art work
required_review: Preserve the historical attribution but record that prior-art work was deferred only for the named campaign and remains required before external novelty claims, unless a durable decision says otherwise.

claim_summary: Session-local GO and signature controls are written as resume requirements without an expiry or current task-state id.
evidence: All first actions are said to need Will's GO
Nothing is canonical until Will signs
The file dates itself and calls itself perishable but does not encode supersession
normative_effect: A later agent may stop valid read-only or mathematical work while waiting on stale permission
required_review: Attach the rule to a dated task-state record with owner and expiry; once stale, retain it as history without normative force.
